rulings.Case Title: STEVEN ADAIR MACDONALD & ASSOCIATES, P.C. VS. GREG NOTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Correct, Nunc Pro Tunc, The Application For And Renewal Of Judgment Filed On November 24, 2020
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, April 16, 2025 Line 1. PLAINTIFF STEVEN ADAIR MACDONALD & ASSOCIATES, P.C.'s Motion To Correct, Nunc Pro Tunc, The Application For And Renewal Of Judgment Filed On November 24, 2020. Plaintiff and Judgment Creditor Steven Adair MacDonald & Associates, P.C.'s Motion To Correct, Nunc Pro Tunc, The Application For And Renewal Of Judgment Filed On November 24, 2020, is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court orders corrected nunc pro tunc the Application for and Renewal of Judgment filed on November 24, 2020 (the "Renewal"), of the judgment entered on December 1, 2010 (the "Judgment") against defendants/judgment debtors GREG NOTO aka GREGORY NOTO dba PLACE PIGALLE also dba MEDICI LOUNGE (collectively, "Judgment Debtors") by adding the date, county, and instrument number of the original Abstract of Judgment recorded with the San Francisco Recorder's Office on January 18, 2011. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 437(d).) The Corrected Application for and Renewal of Judgment ("Corrected Renewal") must be in the form submitted by Judgment Creditor concurrently with this motion as Exhibit F to the supporting declaration of Jessica Taran, sworn to on February 25, 2025. To effectuate the filing of the Corrected Renewal, Plaintiff and Judgment Creditor shall present the Corrected Renewal and this order to the clerk and the clerk shall enter a conforming Corrected Renewal, nunc pro tunc, as of November 24, 2020. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA WARD, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL VS. STT LINES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, April 16, 2025 Line 4. PLAINTIFF CYNTHIA WARD's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement. Plaintiff Cynthia Ward's unopposed Motion for Preliminary Approval of Class Action Settlement is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court: (1) grants preliminary approval of the proposed Class Action Settlement Agreement ("Settlement"); (2) conditionally certifies the proposed Class for settlement purposes only; (3) appoints Plaintiff as the Class Representative; (4) appoints The Ottinger Firm, P.C. as Class Counsel; (5) appoints CPT Group to serve as the Settlement Administrator; (6) approves as to form and content the Notice of Class Action Settlement; and (7) sets a final approval hearing date for November 10, 2025 at 9:00 am in this department, for which Plaintiff shall file a memorandum identifying and discussing outstanding issues no later than October 31, 2025, with courtesy copy delivered to the department within 24 hours of filing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) rulings.Case Title: FIAT GROWTH, INC. VS. GERARD FANE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, April 16, 2025 Line 7. 2 - CROSS DEFENDANTs FIAT GROWTH, INC., FIAT GROWTH, LLC,and FIAT SERVICES, LLC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendants Fiat Growth, Inc., Fiat Growth, LLC and Fiat Services LLC's' motion to strike the prayer for attorney's fees per Labor Code section 218.5 is granted with leave to amend. "Any party, within the time allowed to respond to a pleading may serve and file a notice of motion to strike the whole or any part thereof," (CCP section 435.) "The court may, upon a motion made pursuant to Section 435, or at any time in its discretion, and upon terms it deems proper: [para] (a) Strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading. (b) Strike out all or any part of any pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (CCP section 436.) "In the construction of a pleading, for the purpose of determining its effect, its allegations must be liberally construed, with a view to substantial justice between the parties." (CCP section 452.) (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: RACHEL CROCKETT VS. LLOYD & MOUSILLI, PLLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Propounded To Defendant Lloyd & Mousilli, Pllc
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, April 16, 2025 Line 15. 10 - PLAINTIFFs RACHEL CROCKETT,and DANA KEMPLER's Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Propounded To Defendant Lloyd & Mousilli, Pllc. The TEN discovery motions on calendar in this case are continued to May 15, 2025, at 9:00 am in this Department. The court finds exceptional circumstances support appointment of a discovery referee for all discovery purposes. (CCP 639(a)(5); CRC 3.920(c).) All pending and subsequent discovery matters are so referred. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
